lecture_context.lecture_title: Lecture 9 - Attention, Final Projects and LLM Intro

unit.name: Project Constraints, Resources, and Proposal Requirements
unit.description: Covers topic restrictions, compute options, LLM API resources, and the required elements of the project proposal and paper review.
unit.summary: This segment defines what counts as an acceptable CS224N project and what support students should plan around. Final projects must substantively involve both human language and neural networks, though multimodal combinations are allowed as long as language is central rather than incidental [ts=2770s] [ts=2827s]. The lecturer then reviews realistic compute options—cloud credits, notebook platforms, low-cost GPU services, and Together AI API credits for LLM experiments—while warning that model size strongly affects token budgets and feasibility [ts=2943s] [ts=3155s]. He next explains the project proposal: one per team, centered on both a project plan and a critical two-page research-paper review [ts=3206s] [ts=3260s]. For custom proposals in particular, students must make the data source and evaluation plan concrete, and all proposals now need an ethical-considerations paragraph about deployment risks and mitigations [ts=3308s] [ts=3386s].

key_points.text: A CS224N final project must substantively involve both human language and neural networks.
Cloud and notebook resources are limited, so students should plan projects around realistic compute budgets.
Together AI credits can support experiments on large language models via API access.
The project proposal includes a critical review of a key research paper plus a project plan.
Custom proposals must clearly specify data, evaluation, and an ethical-considerations paragraph.

unit_summary_for_generation: This segment defines what counts as an acceptable CS224N project and what support students should plan around. Final projects must substantively involve both human language and neural networks, though multimodal combinations are allowed as long as language is central rather than incidental [ts=2770s] [ts=2827s]. The lecturer then reviews realistic compute options—cloud credits, notebook platforms, low-cost GPU services, and Together AI API credits for LLM experiments—while warning that model size strongly affects token budgets and feasibility [ts=2943s] [ts=3155s]. He next explains the project proposal: one per team, centered on both a project plan and a critical two-page research-paper review [ts=3206s] [ts=3260s]. For custom proposals in particular, students must make the data source and evaluation plan concrete, and all proposals now need an ethical-considerations paragraph about deployment risks and mitigations [ts=3308s] [ts=3386s].

kp_catalog.name: Project proposal requirements
kp_catalog.description: The proposal combines a critical paper review with a concrete plan covering topic, data, evaluation, and ethical considerations.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because project proposal requirements clarifies the proposal combines a critical paper review with a concrete plan covering topic, data, evaluation, and ethical considerations, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Compute-aware project planning
kp_catalog.description: Project design should respect realistic GPU and API budgets and favor lightweight methods when full-scale training is infeasible.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because compute-aware project planning clarifies project design should respect realistic GPU and API budgets and favor lightweight methods when full-scale training is infeasible, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Default versus custom final project choice
kp_catalog.description: Students may either complete the guided default BERT-based project or define a custom project, with expectations calibrated by team size and scope.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because default versus custom final project choice clarifies students may either complete the guided default BERT-based project or define a custom project, with expectations calibrated by team size and scope, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
